titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Father Carney Gavin Legacy Collection of Holy Land Photographs
Gavin (Father Carney) Legacy Collection of Holy Land Photographs
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday January 23, 2024 at 04:02

did.unittitle: Father Carney Gavin legacy collection of Holy Land photographs

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: In French and English, with some materials in German.

did.abstract: The Father Carney Gavin legacy collection of Holy Land photographs documents early photography of Palestine, Israel, Jordan, Lebanon, Syria, Egypt, and Saudi Arabia in the 1860s and 1870s, as well as early twentieth century archaeological excavations and travel in Iraq, Greece, Egypt, and Palestine. Included are images by M. J. Diness, the Bonfils family, Francis Frith, Abdullah frères, G. Lékégian, J. P. Sébah, and Zangaki. Also present are stereoscopic views of Jerusalem and Palestine produced by Underwood & Underwood in circa 1900. The collection was assembled by Father Carney Gavin, primarily through gifts of photograph collections and single items to the Archives of Historical Documentation, in the 1990s and 2000s. It contains photographic prints, photograph albums, glass lantern slides, cartes-de-visite, stereographs, panoramas, writings and exhibition files, printed material, and electronic and audiovisual media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 55-63 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 64-68 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into nine series: I. Mari-Cha/Rhoné Collection, 1865–1885. II. Mari-Cha/Barnier-Diness Collection, circa 1990–1993. III. Ralph C. Marcove Collection, circa 1860s-1890s. IV. Catherine C. Haynes Collection, 1850–1921. V. Sadiq Bey Collection, 1880. VI. Mathilde Pfeiffer Collection, 1928–1993. VII. Other Photographs and Papers, 1838-1910s, undated. VIII. Carney Gavin Professional Papers, 1820–2013. IX. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1929-circa 2001.

bioghist.head: Carney E. S. Gavin (1939-2014)
bioghist.p: Carney E. S. Gavin, American museum curator, archaeologist, and Catholic priest, was born in Boston in 1939 to Grayce Carney and Patrick Gavin. He received a BA in Classics from Boston College in 1959 and a PhD in Near Eastern languages from Harvard University in 1973. Gavin was Curator of Near Eastern Art and Archaeology at Harvard's Semitic Museum (now the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East) from 1974 to 1993, and he also served as the museum's associate director (1980-1986) and executive director (1986-1993). Throughout his career he worked to locate, catalog, and preserve early Middle Eastern photographs. In 1994 he established the Archives for Historical Documentation (AHD) in Brighton, Massachusetts; one of the organization's primary projects was locating the earliest photographs of the Holy Land. The AHD's collection included prints, cartes-de-visite, photo albums, stereographs, glass lantern slides, and films documenting Middle Eastern cultural heritage. Gavin was ordained as a Catholic priest in Austria in 1965 and served as a longtime pastor for St. Columbkille's Parish in Brighton. He died in 2014.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Father Carney Gavin Legacy Collection of Holy Land Photographs. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are are based on those provided by the creators or previous custodians. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Father Carney Gavin legacy collection of Holy Land photographs documents early photography of Palestine, Israel, Jordan, Lebanon, Syria, Egypt, and Saudi Arabia in the 1860s and 1870s, as well as early twentieth century archaeological excavations and travel in Iraq, Greece, Egypt, and Palestine. Included are images by M. J. Diness, the Bonfils family, Francis Frith, Abdullah frères, G. Lékégian, J. P. Sébah, and Zangaki. Also present are stereoscopic views of Jerusalem and Palestine produced by Underwood & Underwood in circa 1900. The collection was assembled by Father Carney Gavin, primarily through gifts of photograph collections and single items to the Archives of Historical Documentation, in the 1990s and 2000s. It contains photographic prints, photograph albums, glass lantern slides, cartes-de-visite, stereographs, panoramas, writings and exhibition files, printed material, and electronic and audiovisual media.

The bulk of the records consists of six collections: portfolios of albumen prints of street scenes and buildings of Cairo assembled by Arthur Rhoné, author of an early French guidebook for Egypt; modern platinum and silver gelatin prints of Jerusalem, created by photographer John Barnier in the early 1990s from original collodion wet plate negatives of Diness; photograph albums and prints of Egypt, Syria, and Palestine, collected by physician Ralph C. Marcove; cartes-de-visite owned by Catherine C. Haynes, a teacher at Robert College in Istanbul in the 1870s; albumen prints of Mecca and Medina by Muḥammad Ṣādiq Bey, an Egyptian army engineer; and photograph albums and prints assembled by Mathilde Pfeiffer, wife of archaeologist Robert Pfeiffer, showing Harvard University-Baghdad School excavations in Kirkuk, Iraq, and the couple's travels in Egypt, Palestine, and Greece in the 1920s. The Pfeiffer collection also contains film reels of the Holy Land, Baalbek, and Kirkuk, and Cambridge, Massachusetts.

Also included are Ermete Pierotti's Jerusalem explored , two portfolios containing photographs of Mecca and Medina by al-Sayyid 'Abd al-Ghaffār, glass lantern slides depicting the Middle East, the Americas, and Europe, and professional papers of Carney Gavin, as well as film of the Kirkuk excavation, shot by Fogg Art Museum archaeologist Richard F. S. Starr in 1929, and film of travel to Egypt and Palestine in the 1920s.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Father Carney Gavin Legacy Collection of Holy Land Photographs is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: American School of Oriental Research in Baghdad
Archives for Historical Documentation (Brighton, Boston, Mass.)

persname.part: Starr, Richard F. S. (Richard Francis Strong), 1900-

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under four headings: Caire I, Caire II, Caire III, and Portfolio Covers.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Arthur Rhoné wrote one of the earliest tourist guidebooks to Egypt, L'égypte à Petites Journées . He collected and annotated gold-toned photographs of Cairo, working in particular between the years 1865 and 1885, when he systematically arranged the views by each section of the city. Rhoné both purchased and commissioned photographs of Cairo, including multi-part panoramas.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: The materials were previously housed at the Archives for Historical Documentation as part of the Mari-Cha Collections of María Clara Pesantes Becerra and Robert Warren Miller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of three portfolios of annotated panoramas and photographic studies of Cairo and other locations in Egypt, circa 1865-1885, which were assembled by Arthur Rhoné. The portfolios primarily consist of albumen prints depicting panoramic views of Cairo, streets, houses, shops, bazaars, fountains, cemeteries, people, furnishings, woodwork, and fashions. Also included are images of mosques, such as the Mosque-Madrasa of Sultan Hassan, Al-Moayyed Sheikh Mosque, Mosque of Ibn Tulun, Mosque of Kaĭt-bey, and the Mosque of al-Hakim. The series contains a few engravings and drawings by Paul-Jacques-Aimé Baudry. Among the photographers represented are Pascal Sébah, Antonio Beato, Léon & Lévy, Henri Béchard, Émile Béchard, and A. Braun à Dornach. If identified, the name of the photographer is added to titles in brackets. Some plates are missing. Materials were removed from the portfolios and rehoused in folders during processing; the original portfolio covers can be found in box 6.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three portfolio covers that formerly housed the Arthur Rhoné collection. The spines are engraved "Caire I," "Caire II," and "Caire III."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five subseries: 8x10 Prints, Stereoscopic Views, Oversize Prints, Enlarged Lantern Slides, and Other Materials.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Mendel John Diness (1827-1900) was a Jewish watchmaker and early photographer of the Holy Land. He was born in Odessa, Ukraine, and in 1848 moved to Jerusalem, where he converted to Christianity. Diness undertook an extensive photo expedition in Galilee, and he was hired by Italian architect and engineer Ermete Pierotti to photograph Jerusalem as part of a project to survey and restore the Haram al-Sharif. Diness emigrated to the United States in circa 1860 and became a minister and lecturer.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: The materials were previously housed at the Archives for Historical Documentation as part of the Mari-Cha Collections of María Clara Pesantes Becerra and Robert Warren Miller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of platinum and silver prints created by American photographer John Barnier in circa 1990-1993 from original collodion wet plate negatives of M. J. Diness, which Barnier purchased at a tag sale in Minnesota in 1989. The original photographs, dated in the 1850s, depict people, holy sites, monuments, buildings, and landscapes in Jerusalem, Bethlehem, Nazareth, and Safed, as well as surrounding areas such as Mount Zion and the banks of the Jordan River. Most of the images in this series were published in the catalogue Capturing the Holy Land: M.J. Diness and the beginnings of photography in Jerusalem . Titles in quotes were transcribed by Barnier from Diness's own notes; the remaining titles were created by Barnier.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scratched in the plate: bottom left, "M. J. Denis or Deniss"; "Mount of Olives" in the middle, and on the right "Octo. 1858" or "1856"

Non-Diness photographs used for the catalogue, unlabeled negatives and photographs

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Photograph Albums and Portfolios, and Photographic Prints. Within subseries, materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Dr. Ralph C. Marcove, a New York physician specializing in orthopedic oncology, was a friend of Father Carney Gavin and patron of the arts. He assembled the Ralph C. Marcove Collection through the acquisition of more than a dozen Middle Eastern photograph collections in the late 1970s and early 1980s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of albums, portfolios, and groups of loose photographs, primarily albumen prints, depicting Palestine, Egypt, and Syria, as well as Mecca; very few are dated, but all were created in circa 1860s-1890s. Photographers represented include Abdullah frères, Hippolyte Arnoux, Antonio Beato, Francis Bedford, Guillaume Berggren, Félix, Lydie, and Adrien Bonfils, Tancrède Dumas, Frances Frith, Frank Mason (F. M.) Good, Suleiman Hakim, G. Lékégian, Sébah & Joaillier and Jean (J. P.) Sébah, and Zangaki. Included are panoramas, landscapes, street scenes, and images of cemeteries, bazaars, mosques, churches, prisons, monuments, temples, and tombs. Among the structures depicted are the Great Pyramid of Giza, Abu Simbel, the Temple of Ptah, and the Church of the Holy Sepulcher. The series features images of the Nile River, the Anti-Lebanon mountains in Baalbek, and the Valley of Jehoshaphat. Photographs also show children and adults socializing, working, and praying, among other activities.

did.unittitle: Sinai and Palestine , photographed by Francis Frith; 37 albumen prints

Constantinople. View from the Seraskier Tower showing the Golden Horn; Fountain of the Seraglio; Tower of Galata and Portion of a Turkish Cemetery [loose photographs by Bedford]
Brousse. Oulou - Djami; Mouradié. Tombeau des Sultans [loose photographs by Feltel]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All photographs by Bonfils unless otherwise noted.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged by format but otherwise maintain order found at time of acquisition.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Catherine C. Haynes was a teacher at Robert College, the first American-sponsored college founded outside the United States, which opened in Bebek, Turkey in 1863 with four students. The college, founded by Reverend Cyrus Hamlin and financially supported by New York merchant Christopher R. Robert, aimed to educate Christian minorities in the Ottoman Empire.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: The materials were a gift of descendants of Catherine Haynes to the Archives of Historical Documentation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains cartes-de-visite and cabinet cards of students, teachers, and administrators at Robert College in Bebek, Turkey, political figures from the Ottoman Empire, and members of the Haynes family, as well as a few postcards and stereographs. All photographs are by Abdullah frères in Constantinople (now Istanbul), Turkey, unless otherwise noted.

did.unittitle: (Two women, two men in Turkish clothing)
Mr. Penaratoff [Stefan Panaretov], lecturer at Robert College
Sarah Bassett Dobbins [Bijou, Los Angeles, photographer]
M. Haloosd Tirakian [?], professeur de la langue Turque à Robert College

Portrait of unidentified man [Harvey B. Lindsley, Sunbeam Gallery, Auburn, N.Y., photographer]
Bessie Y. Sage, Mary D. Sage, and Margaret W. Sage ("Daisy") [Johnston Wick, photographer]
Portrait of unidentified woman [Stuart, Glasgow, photographer]
Mrs. Van Hook (?) in Persian costume [M. C. Beverage, Marshalltown, Iowa, photographer]
Anna E. Poage, Missy [missionary] in Persia [unidentified photographer]
B. Labaree, Missy [missionary] to Persia [D. K. Prescott, 7 Tremont Row, photographer]
Turkish woman, street dress [P. Sébah, Constantinople, photographer]
Turkish woman, street dress [P. Sébah, Constantinople, photographer]
Portrait of unidentified young man [Hardy, Boston, photographer]
"From Cousoum" Portrait of unidentified young man, aged 16
"To dear Miss Haynes from one of her small boys (?) (6 feet high)" [name illegible]

Scenes from Jerusalem, Palestine, published by Jamal Bros.
Bethlehem women, published by Vester & Co.; Beyrouth - Phare
The Tomb of the Virgin Mary, near Gesthemane, Jerusalem, published by Underwood & Underwood
In the Beautiful Mosque of Omar, Summit of Mt. Moriah, Jerusalem, published by Underwood & Underwood
Kait Bey Mosque, Cairo, Egypt, published by B. L. Singley

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Muḥammad Ṣādiq Bey, an Egyptian army engineer and surveyor, was the first person to take photographs of Mecca, Medina, and the Hajj. Later ranked Lieutenant-General Ṣādiq Pasha, Ṣādiq Bey was an expert cartographer and student of the tribes of the Hijaz (or Hejaz). He oversaw the Khedival Corps de Sanitation, charged with preventing disease during the Pilgrimage, and also served as Amir-al-Hajj, leading North African pilgrims into the Holy Land of Hijaz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographs of Mecca and Medina, as well as Ottoman Empire government officials, including Othman Pascha, general governor of Hijaz.

did.unittitle: Hauptwache in Mekka (im Hintergrunde Çafâ) (small format)
Mekka (die Ka'ba und das Centrum der Moschee)

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: The materials were a gift of Louise A. Pfeiffer to the Archives of Historical Documentation.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Motion picture films from the Mathilde Pfeiffer Collection can be found in Series IX. Computer and Audiovisual Media.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mathilde Pfeiffer accompanied her husband, assyriologist, archaeologist, and biblical scholar Robert H. Pfeiffer on the Harvard-Baghdad School Excavations at Nuzi in Kirkuk, Iraq, during 1928-1929 seasons. This series consists of photo albums and photographic prints of the excavation site and locations visited by the Pfeiffers and their colleagues in Iraq, Egypt, Palestine, and Greece. Images depict local workers assisting with the excavation, antiquities, landscapes, animals, birds, reptiles, cars, boats, and an airplane. Also included are photographs of Bishari people.

did.unittitle: Photograph album, Nuzi archaeological excavations in Iraq: approximately 181 photographs
Travel album, mostly Egypt and Greece; approximately 52 snapshots, 2.25" x 3.5"
Travel album recording journeys in Egypt and Palestine as well as some excavations in Iraq; approximately 72 photographs
Travel album recording journeys in Egypt and Palestine as well as some excavations in Iraq; approximately 72 photographs
"Enlargements of travel photos, 1928-29, plus a small snap of Egypt"; 103 prints, 5" x 7"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains two portfolios of prints based on photographs of Mecca and Medina by physician al-Sayyid 'Abd al-Ghaffār; glass lantern slides depicting the Middle East, the Americas, and Europe; stereographs of Jerusalem and Palestine by Underwood & Underwood; and several panoramas. Also included is Piedmontese military engineer Ermete Pierotti's Jerusalem explored , which consists mainly of photographs by M. J. Diness reproduced in lithograph format; and Julia Pardoe's The beauties of the Bosphorus .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portfolio of collotype prints of Mecca, based on photographs of al-Sayyid 'Abd al-Ghaffār and published by Christiaan Snouck Hurgronje.

In Mekka gebräuchliche Gegenstände'. Printer: Pieter Willem Marinus Trap

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portfolio of twenty photolithographic prints of Mecca, Medina, and surrounding areas, based on photographs of al-Sayyid 'Abd al-Ghaffār and published by Christiaan Snouck Hurgronje.

did.unittitle: Ansicht der Moschee, wärend darin ein gemeinschaftliches CALAT abgehalten wird (vergl. Hierzu MEKKA, Bd. II, S. 88)
Erste Ansicht der Stadt Mekka : links im hintergrund die Festung Djijad. Das grosse Gebaude rechts is tied Hamidijja, links daneben die Druckerei (Missing)
Zweite Ansicht der Stadt Mekka uber die nordwestliche (rechts) und die südwestliche Seite (links) der Moschee hinaus
Dritte Ansicht der Stadt Mekka: links die nördliche Ecke der Moschee:ein wenig südöstlich von der selben das Bas es Salam, durch welches die Moschee eintreten
Die (vor wenigen Jahren errichtete) Drucherei in Mekka
Das Grab der Sittana Mèjumunah und Laager dorthin gepilgerter gepilgerter Mekkaner (verg.) für die Beschreibung des Festes dere Sittana Mejumunah Mekka, Bd. II s 52ff)
Zqeite Ansicht des Lagers der Mejmunapilger
Dritte Ansicht desselben; im Hintergrund windet sich der Weg
Westlicher Theil des sich von Westernnach Osten erstreckenden Thales Muina (Mina) warend der grossen jahrlichen Pilgerversammlung
Die zwischen Munaund 'Arafah gelegene Pilgerstation Muzdal'fah
Der Berg 'Arafah wahrend der Jahrlichen Pilgerversammlung von Suden aus geschen)
Pilgerlager in der Ebene östlich vom 'Arafah-berge

Das Reitkameel (Hèdjin) des Scherif Jahja, eines Sohnes des Scherif Ahmed, dessen Vater der beruhmte 1886 verrbene Grosscherif Abd él-Muttálib (vergl. Uber diesen letzten Konig Mekka's vom alten Schlage Mekka, mit einer reich mit Silbere gestickten Satteldecke (batät). Den Zaum halt der Sklave Jahja's; neben diesen steht im Reiteranzug und links in langen Kleidern zwei Scherife neidrigeren Ranges

Zwei verschiedene Aufnahmen der Rikah. Der Thronsessels, auf welchen man in Mekka die jungfräuliche Braut in der Duchlah-nacht zu erheben ptlegt (vergl, Mekka, Bd. II.8. 167ff). Auf B sitzt der Brautigam dort, wo die Bräutigam dort, wo die Braut sitzen soll
Glass lantern slides of Iraq, Palestine, Egypt, and Greece by M. Cappelli, Milano; microfilm of album by Maxime du Camp

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Slides depict people, antiquities, landscapes, and archaeological excavations, circa 1910s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original M. Cappelli boxes for the glass lantern slides in this subseries, each bearing description in an unknown hand.

did.unittitle: Undated microfilm of an album by Maxime du Camp: "Egypte, Nubie, Palestine et Syrie"
Glass lantern slides by Keystone View Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early 20th century industrial and landscape photographs from Europe, Scandinavia, Greece, and the Americas. Included are images of workers hauling coffee and harvesting bananas, ore mines, dockworkers in Veracruz, and a family standing outside an adobe hut in Mexico; women dancing the hula in Hawaii; an ostrich farm in California; salmon fishermen in Oregon; Port Blakely Mills in Washington; a mining camp in Nevada; Cliff Palace in Colorado; Devils Ink Pot in Yellowstone National Park; cowboys and copper mines in Montana; men building dikes in Illinois; a sponge market in Key West; and women shucking oysters in Baltimore.

did.unittitle: Glass lantern slides by Keystone View Company
Glass lantern slides by Keystone View Company
Glass lantern slides by Keystone View Company
Glass lantern slides by Keystone View Company
Panorama of Es Salt from the northwest, Jordan (unsigned, possibly Tancrède Dumas); carte-de-visite of young woman by Félix Bonfils
Pardoe, Miss (Julia). The beauties of the Bosphorus: illustrated in a series of views of Constantinople and its environs, from original drawings by W.H. Bartlett
Pierotti, Ermete. Jerusalem explored, being a description of the ancient and modern city, with numerous illustrations consisting of views ground plans, and sections , vol. 2
Sébah, Pascal. Panorama de Constantinople pris de la tour du Séraskierat par Sébah & Joaillier

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles are transcribed from individual stereographs.

did.unittitle: Eastern Series. Stereoscopic Views. Pool of Siloam
Eastern Series. Stereoscopic Views. The town of Tiberias, Sea of Galilee
9. The Tower of David--from outside the City Wall--Jerusalem, Palestine
11. Jerusalem, "The City of Zion"--southwest from the northern Wall--Palestine
12. Jerusalem and Mount of Olives--east from the Latin Hospice--Palestine
13. Cattle Market Day, in the Lower Pool of Gihon, Valley of Hinnom, Jerusalem, Palestine (Isaiah xxii : 9)
14. The Valley of Kedron and the Village of Siloam--from the south--Palestine
15. The Pool of Siloam--outside of Jerusalem, Palestine (St. John ix : 1-7)
16. Tombs of the Prophets, in the King's Dale, Valley of Kedron, Jerusalem, Palestine
17. Garden of Gethsemane and Mount of Olives--from the eastern Wall--Jerusalem, Palestine
18. Jerusalem, the City of the Great King, from the Mount of Olives, Palestine
19. Christian Street--motley Life in the Holy City's bazaar district--Jerusalem, Palestine
20. The Church of the Holy Sepulchre, Jerusalem, Palestine
22. Easter Procession of the Greek Patriarch, entering the Church of the Holy Sepulchre, Jerusalem, Palestine
23. Pilgrims on the Villa Dolorosa, "the route to Cavalry," Jerusalem, Palestine
24. The beautiful Church of the Armenian Christians, Jerusalem, Palestine
25. The Jews' Wailing Place, outer Wall of Solomon's Temple, Jerusalem, Palestine (Ps. lxxix)
26. "The New Cavalry," Outside the Damascus Gate--from the northern Wall--Jerusalem, Palestine
27. "The Tomb of Our Lord," "The New Cavalry," outside of Jerusalem, Palestine
28. A Tomb with the Stone rolled away (Tomb of the Kings), Jerusalem, Palestine
29. Damascus Gate, the northern Entrance to Jerusalem, Palestine
30. The "Dome of the Rock," site of Solomon's Temple--from the northwest--Jerusalem, Palestine
31. The Sacred Rock, where the Temple Altar stood, Mt. Moriah, Jerusalem, Palestine (II Chron. iii : 1)
32. The Pulpit of Omar, Mosque of el-Aksa, Jerusalem, Palestine
33. The lower Road to Bethany, southeast from Jerusalem, Palestine
34. "Unclean ! Unclean !" Wretched Lepers outside Jerusalem, Palestine (St. Matt. xiii : 2-4)
35. Ancient Olive Trees, Garden of Gethsemane, near Jerusalem, Palestine (St. Matt. xxvi : 36-46)
1. Jaffa, the Joppa of Bible times, Palestine
2. The bazaar of Jaffa on a market day, Palestine
3. A caravan of camels, in the narrow road approaching Jaffa, Palestine
4. Plowing in the fertile Plain of Sharon, Lydda
5. Pass of upper Beth-horon from S., scene of Hebrew victories, Palestine
6. Looking S. E. from Mizpah to Jerusalem four miles away
8. Damascus Gate in N. wall: S. past Church of Holy Sepulchre, Jerusalem
10. Jerusalem on the west--view S. from new tower over the walls
11. The Valley of Kedron and Village of Siloam, Palestine
12. Tombs of the Prophets, in the King's Dale, Valley of Kedron, Jerusalem, Pal.
14. Ancient olive trees, Garden of Gethsemane, near Jerusalem, Palestine (St. Matt. xxvi : 35-46)
15. "Unclean! Unclean!" wretched lepers outside Jeruslaem, Palestine (St. Matt. Viii; 2-4)
16. Jerusalem, the City of the Great King, from Mount of Olives, Palestine
17. Gethsemane road to Stephen's Gate, thronged with pilgrims; west from Olivet
18. The "Dome of the Rock" where the Temple Altar stood, Mt. Moriah, Jerusalem, Palestine
19. The sacred Rock, where the Temple Altar stood, Mt. Moriah, Jerusalem (II Chron. iii : x)
20. Pilgrims in the Temple area; N. from El Aksa to Dome of the Rock, Jerusalem
21. The Jews' wailing place, outer wall of Solomon's Temple, Jerusalem, Palestine (Ps. lxxix)
22. Inside a Jewish synagogue, showing Holy place and reader's platform, Jerusalem
24. Christian Street--motely life in the Holy City's bazaar district, Jerusalem
25. Church of the Holy Sepulchre, Jerusalem
27. Easter procession of Greek patriarch, entering Church of Holy Sepulchre, Jerusalem
28. "The New Cavalry," outside Damascus Gate, from the northern wall
29. "The Tomb of our Lord," "New Cavalry," outside of Jerusalem, Palestine
30. Jerusalem and the Mount of Olives--east from the Latin Hospice, Palestine
31. Jerusalem, the centre of Christian history, seen from west tower on Olivet, Pal.
32. From Olivet east over the Wilderness, Jordan Valley and Dead Sea to Moab, Pal.
33. Bethany, where our Lord was anointed by Mary--S. from eastern slope of Olivet--Palestine
34. David's city, Bethlehem, S. E. to Frank Mountain, Palestine
35. Bethlehem of Judea, the birthplace of Jesus--west from the Church of Nativity, Palestine
36. Church of Nativity, built where Jesus was born, Bethlehem of Judea
38. A shepherd in David's home country leading flock over the Judean hills, Pal.
39. Hebron, the home of Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, from the east--Palestine
40. Mosque Machpelah, the burial place of Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, Hebron, Pal.
41. Gaza, lowland stronghold of the Philistines, from the southeast, Palestine
42. Soene of Goliath's defeat, valley of Elah (N. W.), Palestine
43. Sheikh El Raschid and his escorts, famous Bedouins of Palestine, Bethpage, (S. W.)
45. Plain of the Jordan--southeast from the ruins of ancient Jericho
46. Ruins of Jericho, showing buildings (left) and city wall (right), Palestine
47. Baptizing in the Jordan, Palestine--(St. Matt. Iii; 5. 6)
48. On the north shore of the Dead Sea--looking S. W.--Palestine
49. Mt. Nebo, S. from Wady Ayun Musa, showing road to Jordan, Syria
50. Jerash (Gerasa) in eastern Gilead; looking northward over Roman ruins, Syria
51. Old road from Jericho to Ai (west), among hills of Benjamin, Palestine
52. From Ramah, traditional home of Samuel, N. E. toward Gilead, Pal.
53. Little folks studying at the village school in Samuel's home town, Ramah, Pal.
54. Women grinding at the mill--Palestine (St. Matt. xxiv : 41)
55. Bread-making in the Court of a Syrian home, Palestine
56. Gathering tares from wheat in the stony fields of Bethel, Palestine (looking south)
57. Shiloh, scene of old religious assemblies, from the southeast, Palestine
58. Gerizim and hills of southern Samaria; S. from Mt. Ebal over Sychar, Pal.
59. Mt. Gerizim where Samaritans worshiped, S. W. from entrance to Jacob's well, Pal.
60. A Samaritan woman at Jacob's Well, Palestine (John iv : 5-30)
61. The Passover as celebrated by the Samaritans on Mount Gerizim, Pal.
62. Shechem, an early centre of Hebrew history, looking S. W. from Mt. Ebal
63. Samaritan High Priest and Pentateuch roll--Shechem, Palestine
65. Samaria's magnificent temple with altar at farther end, seen from S. W., Palestine
66. Fertile plain of Dothan, S. W. from hill where the town stood in Elisha's day, Pal.
67. Caesaria, where Paul was tried before Felix, Festus and Agrippa, (N.) Palestine
68. Haifa and the Bay of Akka, east from Mt. Carmel, Palestine (I Kings xviii : 42-46)
69. Rock of Elijah's altar on Mt. Carmel, and plain of Esdraelon, Palestine (I Kings xvii : 20)
70. The Mound of Magiddo from the southeast, Samaria, Palestine
71. Four thousand years unchanged--patriarchal life in Palestine today
72. North from Gilboa over Jezreel plain to the Sea of Galilee, Palestine
73. Gideon's battlefield and Hill of Moreh, N. from Jezreel, Palestine
74. Broad sunny plain of Esdraelon and Mt. Carmel, west from Mt. Gilboa, Pal.
75. From "Little Hermon" N. W. across Esdraelon Plain to Nazareth, Palestine
76. Village of Nain and Mt. Tabor--looking N. E.--Palestine (St. Luke xii : 11-16)
77. Beautiful Nazareth; outlook S. from hills above town to historic Esdraelon, Pal.
78. Ancient "Fountain of the Virgin," where Mary came for water--Nazareth, Palestine
79. A threshing-floor in the hills of Galilee-- the women winnowing--Palestine
80. Cana of Galilee and its well--from the south--Palestine (John ii : 1-10)
81. In the court of a village home--Cana of Galilee, Palestine
82. Looking N. E. from Mount of Beatitudes to Sea of Galilee, Palestine (St. Matt. v : 1)
83. The storied Sea of Galilee and its wall of hills; N. over Tiberias on west bank, Pal.
84. Tiberias, a town of Jewish fisherman. Sea of Galilee, Palestine (from the southeast)
85. Life on the shore of Galilee, at Tiberias, Palestine
86. Fisherman on the Sea of Galilee and distant hills of the Gadarenes, Palestine
87. Plain of Gennesaret and the Sea, N. from above Magdala to Upper Galilee, Pal.
88. Tabighah spring and Gennesaret plain; view S.W. to Horns of Hattin, Pal.
89. Traditional Capernaum, Christ's home by the Sea of Galilee--from the N. W.--Palestine
90. Fisherman mending nets at Jordan's entrance to Galilee: (view S.) Pal.
91. West over sea of Galilee, from above Kersa (Gergesa) to Horns of Hattin, Palestine
92. Plain of the Upper Jordan, N. from near Lake Huleh to Mt. Hermon, Pal.
93. Down the upper Jordan Valley, S. W. from Caesarea Philippi on Mt. Hermon, Pal.
94. In beautiful old Lebanon, E. over upper Jordan Valley to Mt. Hermon, Palestine
95. Ruins of ancient Tyre--wonderful fulfillment of prophecy (Ezekiel xxvi : xxvii)--Syria
96. Ancient citadel in the sea, at Sidon, Syria (Acts xxvi : 1-4)
97. The Oldest of the Sacred Cedars of Lebanon--Monarch of the 400
98. The mightiest building stone ever cut, ruins of Baalbek, Syria
99. Damascus and its gardens--from the northwest-Syria
100. An oriental room on two levels, where honored guests "go up higher", Damascus
"Jerusalem through the Stereoscope" [booklet found in box with stereographs]
Empty boxes formerly housing two sets of stereographs, labeled "Jerusalem" and "Palestine Part 1 and Part 2"

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five subseries: Mari-Cha/Barnier-Diness Materials, Mari-Cha/Rhoné Materials, Bilder Aus Mekka Materials, Other Papers, and Printed Materials. Within subseries, materials maintain order found at time of acquisition unless otherwise noted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials related to the Carney Gavin's work at the Harvard Semitic Museum and Archives for Historical Documentation, including the exhibition and publication of photographs from M. J. Diness, the Arthur Rhoné collection, Bilder Aus Mekka , and Bonfils. Also present are a small amount of Gavin's personal papers and printed materials, such as articles written by or about Gavin, postcards, and posters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains research by John Barnier, copies of platinum prints created from Diness's original collodion wet plate negatives, and materials related to the 1993 exhibition and catalogue, Capturing the Holy Land: M. J. Diness and the Beginnings of Photography in Jerusalem .

did.unittitle: Diness diary entries, photocopies [John Barnier research materials]
John's list & numbers - title list of Barnier's prints from glass negatives
Platinum print demonstration by John Barnier
Photographs of exhibition and unidentified cemetery
Silver and platinum prints, including Dennis [Diness] family
Photocopies of images used in catalogue
Front side to be printed [photographs appearing in catalogue]
Panorama of Jerusalem by M. J. Diness, given to Gavin by John Barnier [platinum print, removed from frame]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Created from Diness's original negatives by John Barnier in 1990-1996. Includes Barnier's handwritten captions in pencil.

did.unittitle: Plates 40, 69, 73, 82, and enlargement from lantern slide 14 (Nazareth)
Diness: 4x5 color transparencies, 4x5 b&w negatives, 35 mm b&w negatives, 35 mm color transparencies
The Mari-Cha/Rhoné Collection Slides: may be projected
The Mari-Cha/Rhoné Collection Slides: master set (do not project)
"Historic Cairo: A Preview," prepared for the United Nations
"Seven Tales of Cairo: A Photographic Narrative on the Eve of Modernity," a preliminary study for a publication and exhibit by the Archives for Historical Documentation
Collection inventory and copies of writings by Arthur Rhoné

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Replicas of black-and-white matted prints and text from Bilder Aus Mekka .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes a small amount of materials related to Bonfils, two diaries by an unknown author living in Israel, a few drawings and maps, and a certificate of merit given to Carney Gavin by Jerusalem mayor Ehud Olmert.

did.unittitle: 8x10 b&w negatives, Bonfils and others; 4x5 color transparencies
Copy photos of Ales and Vicinity, gift of the city of Ales, August 1993 (Also: Photos of Bonfils descendants)
Copy photos of Ales and Vicinity, gift of the city of Ales, August 1993 (Also: Photos of Bonfils descendants) [includes notes and drawing]
Certificate of Merit from Jerusalem Mayor Ehud Olmert to Carney Gavin
Diary - Galilee, Jerusalem, Cedars of Barouk [author unknown, possibly Joseph Goodhew or Goodhue]
Daily Diary [author unknown, possibly Joseph Goodhew or Goodhue]
Etching of a panoramic view by Ali Bey el Abbassi
Mecca al-Mukarrama, laminated map of panorama

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two subheadings: Posters and Exhibition Ephemera, and Publications. Arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: "Legacy of Light/L'Heritage lumineux," materials related to exhibition and conference at the University of Jordan in 1980
Boston College Burns Library - "Jerusalem Pictorial and Descriptive: The Holy City in 19th Century Literature" exhibition poster
"Jerusalem Revealed," unpublished catalogue for exhibition at Regis College
Gavin, Carney E. S. "Bonfils and the Early Photography of the Near East," offprint from Harvard library bulletin
Remembrances of the Near East: The Photographers of Bonfils, 1867-1907 [exhibition catalogue]
Gavin, Carney E. S., Elizabeth Carella, and Ingborg O'Reilly. "The Photographers Bonfils of Beirut and Ales," Camera
Gavin, Carney E. S. The Image of the East: Photographs of Bonfils from the Harvard Semitic Museum
"Moments of Light: Photographs of the Middle East by Felix Bonfils," Polaroid close-Up

"The Bonfils Collection: A Story of Photo-Archaeology," offprint from Saudi
Aramco World
Gavin, Carney E. S. "The earliest voices from the East: photo-archaeological exploration and tomorrow's museums," offprint from Museum [cover contains Gavin's signature and AHD sticker]
Photocopied chapter from The Invention of photography and its impact on learning
Capturing the Holy Land: M.J. Diness and the beginnings of photography in Jerusalem [exhibition catalogue]
"A Legacy of Light," Saudi Aramco World

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Computer Media, and Audiovisual Media.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of electronic media produced by the Archives for Historical Documentation, and audiovisual materials such as reel-to-reel tapes and other video and sound recordings.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two subheadings: Motion Picture Films, and Cassette Tapes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains films depicting Palestine, Egypt, Greece, and the Nuzi archaeological expedition in Kirkuk, Iraq in 1929, as well as cassette tapes related to Dr. Laila 'Ali Ibrahim.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 64-68 (Audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: Film is silent and in black and white. Built-in frame movement. Debris in camera gate. Existing and opening splices, oxidation marks, and perforation damage and tears.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: Film is silent. Black and white film. Existing and opening splices, perforation damage. Shots filmed out of focus. Debris in camera gate. Built-in frame movement.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Film no. 3 Alexandria, Cairo, Royal Funeral, Acrobatics
did.materialspec: Film is silent. black and white. Existing splices. Moisture damage and oxidation marks. Built-in frame movement. Debris in camera gate.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: Film is silent. Color reversal. Existing and opening splices. Perforation damage and burnt frames. Built-in frame movement. Color balance varies. Some shots filmed in slow motion.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Films depict the Holy Land, Baalbek, and Kirkuk, as well as Cambridge, Massachusetts.
